title: A cascade hole-transfer strategy towards stable hybrid perovskite solar cells - Nature Energy
text:
Perovskite solar cells are a leading candidate for next-generation commercial photovoltaics, but their sensitivity to heat and moisture limits their practical use. Now, a cascade hole-transfer strategy is developed to suppress charge recombination in hybrid perovskite/organic solar cells while offering intrinsic moisture stability.

Tai, Q. et al. Efficient and stable perovskite solar cells prepared in ambient air irrespective of the humidity. Nat. Commun. 7, 11105 (2016). This study showed that thiocyanate-incorporated perovskites can be processed in humid ambient air, highlighting an early strategy to mitigate moisture-related instability in PSCs.Article

Lee, M.-H. et al. Suppressing hole accumulation through sub‐nanometer dipole interfaces in hybrid perovskite/organic solar cells for boosting near‐infrared photon harvesting. Adv. Mater. 36, e2411015 (2024). This study demonstrated that HSCs can enhance near-infrared harvesting, while highlighting the importance of electronic-structure control to suppress hole accumulation at the perovskite–organic interface.Article
